description:
You are a Linux systems engineer who has been asked to create a very small
    “lab-hardening” automation in your own home directory.  
    Three sample configuration files have already been placed in
    /home/user/hardening_test/ for you to practise on.  
    Your goal is to write ONE self-contained POSIX-sh compatible script named

        /home/user/hardening_test/apply_hardening.sh

    that performs all of the actions listed below using a *total* of
    **between 5 and 10 individual shell commands** (comments and blank lines
    do not count as commands).

    Automation requirements
    1. Safety backup  
       • Before touching any file, create a backup copy in the same directory  
         using the suffix “.orig”.  
         Examples: sshd_config → sshd_config.orig

    2. Idempotent line insertion  
       • For each target file, make sure the indicated hardening line is
         present exactly once at the very end of the file (append only when
         it is absent).  
           - /home/user/hardening_test/sshd_config  
             line to enforce:  PermitRootLogin no  
           - /home/user/hardening_test/sysctl.conf  
             line to enforce:  net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0  
           - /home/user/hardening_test/login.defs  
             line to enforce:  PASS_MAX_DAYS   90

    3. Structured logging  
       • After processing the three files, the script must create a plaintext
         log file

           /home/user/hardening_test/hardening.log

         containing exactly three lines—one per file—using the following
         fixed format (no extra spaces, uppercase keywords):

             <FILENAME>:<ACTION>

         where  
           <FILENAME> is the basename (e.g., sshd_config) and  
           <ACTION>   is either UPDATED (line was appended) or SKIPPED
                       (line already existed so nothing changed).

         Example of a fully UPDATED run:  
             sshd_config:UPDATED  
             sysctl.conf:UPDATED  
             login.defs:UPDATED

    4. User feedback  
       • When the script finishes, it must print the single line

             Hardening completed

         to STDOUT (nothing else).

    5. Re-runnability  
       • Running the script a second time must leave the files unchanged
         and must write SKIPPED in hardening.log for every file.

    Additional constraints
    • The script must be executable (chmod +x).  
    • Use only shell built-ins and common GNU/Linux user-land tools that are
      present in a minimal distribution (e.g., cat, grep, cp, echo).  
    • You are limited to 5–10 terminal commands to build, permission, test,
      and run the script; feel free to combine actions with && or ; where
      necessary.  
    • Do not edit files outside /home/user/hardening_test/.  
    • Ensure that the final state exactly meets the specifications so that
      an automated grader can validate:

        - presence of *.orig backups (byte-for-byte copy of the originals),
        - the three configuration files ending with their respective hardening
          lines (no duplicates),
        - a correctly formatted hardening.log,
        - the “Hardening completed” message is printed to STDOUT.

    Tip: the grader will run your script twice to confirm idempotency.
truth:
Initial files present before the task begins:

    1. /home/user/hardening_test/sshd_config
       Contents (7 lines):
         # Sample OpenSSH daemon configuration for the lab
         Port 22
         Protocol 2
         HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
         HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key
         HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key
         # End of file

    2. /home/user/hardening_test/sysctl.conf
       Contents (4 lines):
         # Kernel sysctl configuration
         vm.swappiness = 10
         fs.file-max = 65535
         # End of file

    3. /home/user/hardening_test/login.defs
       Contents (5 lines):
         # Shadow password suite configuration
         UID_MIN                  1000
         UID_MAX                 60000
         UMASK                    077
         # End of file


    Expected final state *after the script is run once*:

    Backups (exact copies of originals):
      /home/user/hardening_test/sshd_config.orig
      /home/user/hardening_test/sysctl.conf.orig
      /home/user/hardening_test/login.defs.orig

    Modified files (original lines plus the enforced line appended):
      /home/user/hardening_test/sshd_config
        … previous 7 lines …
        PermitRootLogin no

      /home/user/hardening_test/sysctl.conf
        … previous 4 lines …
        net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0

      /home/user/hardening_test/login.defs
        … previous 5 lines …
        PASS_MAX_DAYS   90

    Log file:
      /home/user/hardening_test/hardening.log
      EXACT contents (3 lines, each with trailing newline):
        sshd_config:UPDATED
        sysctl.conf:UPDATED
        login.defs:UPDATED

    Standard output from running apply_hardening.sh the first time:
        Hardening completed\n

    Standard output from running apply_hardening.sh the second time:
        Hardening completed\n

    Log file after second run (idempotency check):
        sshd_config:SKIPPED
        sysctl.conf:SKIPPED
        login.defs:SKIPPED
